question_text: Why does a drawing pin have a sharp point and a flat head?

options.label: A
options.text: To reduce the force needed to push it in

options.label: B
options.text: To increase the total force applied

options.label: C
options.text: To spread the pressure over a large area

options.label: D
options.text: To make it easier to hold

correct_answer: To reduce the force needed to push it in
explanation: A drawing pin works because pressure = force ÷ area. The sharp point has a very small area, so even a small force creates high pressure, allowing it to pierce surfaces easily. The flat head has a large area to distribute the force when pushing, protecting your finger.
